name: Catfish Creole
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut fillets into 1-inch pieces.
2. Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
3. Add flour to the oil and stir until it forms a paste, about 2-3 minutes.
4. Remove the flour mixture from the pan and gradually add water while stirring continuously until well combined.
5. Return the flour mixture to the pan and stir in celery, shallots, bell pepper, garlic, tomatoes, tomato sauce, salt, bay leaves, thyme, pepper, brown sugar, lemon juice, parsley, and Worcestershire sauce.
6. Stir in Tabasco sauce and cook for a few more minutes until the vegetables are wilted.
7. Add catfish pieces to the pan and simmer for 8-10 minutes or until fish flakes easily with a fork.
8. Serve hot over rice in soup bowls.
